name: ChatGPT
tagline: Conversational AI for writing, research, and problem-solving
bestFor: Essays, brainstorming, and explaining complex topics
uniHack: Use the system prompt to set yourself as a student needing step-by-step explanations
description: ChatGPT is OpenAI's flagship conversational AI, capable of drafting essays, solving math problems, debugging code, and explaining complex concepts. Students use it daily for homework help, study summaries, and creative projects. The free tier provides solid functionality, while Plus unlocks GPT-4o for advanced tasks.

name: Claude
tagline: Thoughtful AI assistant with deep reasoning and long-context support
bestFor: Long essays, document analysis, and nuanced reasoning
uniHack: Upload your entire textbook chapter and ask Claude to quiz you or summarize key concepts
description: Claude by Anthropic excels at nuanced writing, thorough analysis, and handling very long documents in a single conversation. It is known for careful reasoning and safety-conscious responses, making it ideal for academic work. Students appreciate its ability to read and summarize entire research papers or textbooks.

name: Grammarly
tagline: AI-powered grammar, style, and plagiarism checker
pricingDetail: Free basic; Premium ~$12/month, student discounts available
bestFor: Polishing essays and checking grammar in real time
uniHack: Install the browser extension to get feedback directly inside Google Docs and Canvas
description: Grammarly is an AI writing assistant that catches grammar mistakes, suggests clearer phrasing, and checks for plagiarism. Its browser extension integrates seamlessly into students' existing workflows across email, docs, and LMS platforms. The premium plan provides genre-specific writing style guidance and citation assistance.

name: Notion AI
tagline: AI-enhanced note-taking and knowledge management
bestFor: Organizing lecture notes and generating study wikis
uniHack: Create a class dashboard in Notion and let AI auto-summarize your meeting notes into action items
description: Notion AI integrates generative AI directly into the popular all-in-one workspace, helping students draft notes, summarize lengthy readings, and generate structured study plans. It lets you chat with your entire Notion database to quickly surface important information. The free tier is generous for individual students.

name: Quillbot
tagline: AI paraphrasing, summarization, and citation tool
bestFor: Paraphrasing academic text and generating citations
uniHack: Use the summarizer on dense research papers before reading to understand what you are getting into
description: QuillBot helps students rephrase complex academic writing into clearer prose and automatically generates citations in APA, MLA, or Chicago format. The summarizer can condense long journal articles into key bullet points in seconds. It also integrates with Google Docs and Microsoft Word for seamless editing.

name: GitHub Copilot
tagline: AI pair programmer that autocompletes code in real time
pricingDetail: Free for students via GitHub Education; $10/month otherwise
bestFor: Coding assignments and project development
uniHack: Apply for the GitHub Student Developer Pack to get Copilot completely free
description: GitHub Copilot is an AI pair programmer built into VS Code, JetBrains, and other popular editors that suggests code completions in real time. It helps students move past syntax barriers and learn new languages faster by showing contextually relevant code examples. Students enrolled in the GitHub Education program get it free.

name: Cursor
tagline: AI-first code editor with chat, autocomplete, and codebase understanding
bestFor: Building full-stack projects with AI assistance throughout
uniHack: Use the Ctrl+K inline edit shortcut to refactor messy assignment code without switching context
description: Cursor is a fork of VS Code built with AI at its core, enabling students to chat with their entire codebase, generate multi-file edits, and fix bugs by describing them in plain English. Its composer feature can scaffold entire applications from a short description. The free Hobby tier provides generous monthly AI completions.

name: Replit AI
tagline: Browser-based coding environment with built-in AI assistant
bestFor: Coding directly in the browser without setup
uniHack: Share a Replit link with classmates to collaborate on coding assignments in real time
description: Replit lets students code in over 50 programming languages directly in the browser without any local setup. Its integrated AI assistant can explain errors, generate boilerplate, and suggest improvements. Real-time multiplayer coding makes it perfect for pair programming on group assignments.

name: Tabnine
tagline: Privacy-first AI code completion for every IDE
bestFor: Code autocomplete with strong privacy controls
uniHack: Enable whole-line completions in your campus-approved IDE for faster coding assignments
description: Tabnine provides AI-powered code completion that runs locally or in the cloud and integrates with virtually every major IDE. Its privacy-first architecture is especially useful at universities with strict data policies. The free tier covers most student needs with accurate single and multi-line suggestions.

name: Codeium
tagline: Free AI coding assistant with autocomplete and chat
bestFor: Free alternative to Copilot for student projects
uniHack: Install Codeium in VS Code for free Copilot-level completions with no credit card required
description: Codeium offers fast, accurate AI code completions and an in-editor chat assistant completely free for individual developers and students. It supports more than 70 programming languages and integrates with VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, and others. It is a popular choice for students who cannot access paid alternatives.

name: Perplexity AI
tagline: AI search engine with cited real-time answers
bestFor: Getting sourced answers to research questions instantly
uniHack: Use the Focus > Academic mode to restrict results to scholarly sources like arXiv and PubMed
description: Perplexity AI acts as an AI-powered research engine that provides direct answers to questions with citations to real web sources. Students can use its Academic focus mode to surface peer-reviewed papers and academic content. Every answer includes clickable source links, making fact-checking straightforward.

name: Elicit
tagline: AI research assistant for finding and summarizing papers
bestFor: Literature reviews and systematic research synthesis
uniHack: Paste your research question and let Elicit pull the top 20 papers with auto-extracted key findings
description: Elicit is designed for academic research, helping students find relevant papers, extract key data from studies, and map the literature around any topic. Its semantic search goes beyond keyword matching to surface conceptually related work. Researchers and students use it to conduct systematic reviews far faster than manually.

name: Consensus
tagline: Search engine that finds scientific consensus on research questions
bestFor: Quickly finding what scientific evidence says on a topic
uniHack: Ask a yes/no research question to see what percentage of studies support or refute the claim
description: Consensus searches over 200 million scientific papers and aggregates findings into a consensus meter showing how much the research agrees or disagrees on a topic. Students can type a research question in plain English and get evidence-backed summaries instead of raw search results. It is especially useful for health, psychology, and social science topics.

name: Semantic Scholar
tagline: Free AI-powered academic search engine with paper insights
bestFor: Finding and understanding academic papers with AI insights
uniHack: Use the TLDR feature to get a two-sentence summary of any paper before deciding to read the full version
description: Semantic Scholar is a free academic search engine powered by AI that covers over 200 million papers across all fields. Its AI-generated TLDR feature gives students a quick summary of any paper, while citation graphs help them trace how ideas evolve across the literature. Entirely free, making it ideal for budget-conscious students.

name: SciSpace
tagline: AI copilot for reading and understanding research papers
bestFor: Understanding dense research papers with AI explanations
uniHack: Highlight any confusing paragraph and click Ask to get an instant plain-English explanation
description: SciSpace lets students upload research papers or find them via search and then ask the AI copilot questions about the content inline. It can explain complex equations, interpret tables, and suggest related papers for deeper reading. The tool is especially helpful for students entering a new research area who encounter unfamiliar jargon.

name: Connected Papers
tagline: Visual graph of connected research papers for literature exploration
bestFor: Visualizing how papers relate and finding seminal works
uniHack: Start with one key paper to generate a graph and instantly spot the 5 most-cited foundational works
description: Connected Papers generates an interactive visual graph of papers related to a seed paper, showing how research fields branch and connect. Students use it to quickly identify the seminal works in a field and discover derivative research they might have missed. The graph clusters help visualize sub-topics within a research area.

name: Anki
tagline: Spaced repetition flashcard system for long-term memory retention
pricingDetail: Free on desktop and Android; $25 one-time on iOS
bestFor: Memorizing vocabulary, formulas, and facts with proven science
uniHack: Import community decks for your medical school, bar exam, or language class to save card-making time
description: Anki is the gold-standard spaced repetition flashcard app used by medical students, language learners, and professionals worldwide. Its algorithm schedules card reviews at optimal intervals to maximize long-term memory retention with minimum study time. Thousands of community-shared decks cover virtually every subject students need.

name: Quizlet
tagline: AI-powered flashcards and study modes for any subject
bestFor: Creating and studying flashcard sets with AI explanations
uniHack: Use the Q-Chat feature to have the AI quiz you conversationally instead of clicking through cards
description: Quizlet is one of the most popular study platforms among students, combining AI-powered flashcard creation with interactive study modes like Learn, Spell, and Match. The Q-Chat AI tutor engages students conversationally to reinforce concepts. Millions of pre-made sets cover everything from AP exams to university-level courses.

name: Khan Academy Khanmigo
tagline: AI tutor that guides students through problems without giving answers away
bestFor: Math tutoring and concept mastery through guided practice
uniHack: When stuck on a proof, ask Khanmigo to ask you leading questions rather than showing the solution
description: Khanmigo is Khan Academy's GPT-4-powered AI tutor that teaches through guided questioning rather than simply giving answers. It is designed for K-12 through introductory college subjects and is deeply integrated with Khan Academy's curriculum and practice exercises. The platform is free or donation-based, making it one of the most accessible AI tutors available.

name: Photomath
tagline: Snap a math problem and get step-by-step solutions instantly
pricingDetail: Free basic; Plus $10/month for animated explanations
bestFor: Understanding math problem-solving steps from a photo
uniHack: Don't just copy the answer — read through each step to understand the method for your exam
description: Photomath lets students photograph any handwritten or printed math problem and instantly receive detailed step-by-step solutions. It supports arithmetic through calculus and explains each step clearly, helping students understand methods rather than just copying answers. The Plus plan adds animated explanations and expert-created problem walkthroughs.

name: Wolfram Alpha
tagline: Computational knowledge engine for math, science, and more
bestFor: Solving complex math, chemistry, and physics problems computationally
uniHack: Type 'solve x^2+5x+6=0 step by step' to see full derivation instead of just the answer
description: Wolfram Alpha is a computational knowledge engine that can solve calculus problems, balance chemical equations, interpret statistical data, and answer scientific questions with precise, sourced answers. Students in STEM fields rely on it for complex problem-solving beyond basic calculators. Its Pro plan provides detailed step-by-step solutions for studying.

name: Socratic by Google
tagline: AI homework helper that explains concepts visually
bestFor: Getting visual explanations to homework questions via camera
uniHack: Take a photo of your homework question to get curated video explanations linked to the topic
description: Socratic by Google uses AI to identify questions from photos and surface the best explanations, videos, and resources available online. It covers math, science, English, and history for middle and high school students. The visual and video-focused approach suits students who learn better from diagrams than from text.

name: Todoist AI
tagline: Smart task manager with AI natural language scheduling
bestFor: Organizing assignments and deadlines with natural language
uniHack: Type 'submit chemistry lab every Friday at 11pm' and AI will create recurring reminders automatically
description: Todoist uses AI to parse natural language task entries, automatically setting due dates, priorities, and recurring schedules. Students can dump all their assignments in plain text and let the app organize them by priority and deadline. Cross-device sync and calendar integration keep academic schedules consistent everywhere.

name: Motion
tagline: AI scheduler that automatically plans your day around your tasks
bestFor: Auto-scheduling study blocks around classes and deadlines
uniHack: Add all assignments as tasks with deadlines and let Motion block out study time automatically
description: Motion uses AI to automatically build and rebuild your daily schedule based on your tasks, deadlines, meetings, and personal work style. Students can input all upcoming deadlines and the app will allocate focused study blocks across the calendar, automatically rescheduling when conflicts arise. It reduces the cognitive load of planning exam prep periods.

name: Otter.ai
tagline: AI meeting and lecture transcription with real-time notes
bestFor: Transcribing lectures and study group discussions automatically
uniHack: Enable Otter on your phone during lecture and review the searchable transcript instead of handwritten notes
description: Otter.ai provides real-time transcription of lectures, study sessions, and video calls, producing searchable, speaker-labeled notes. Its AI generates automatic summaries so students can review key points without rewatching entire recordings. Integrations with Zoom and Google Meet make it easy to capture any online class.

name: Midjourney
tagline: Leading AI image generator for stunning artistic visuals
bestFor: Creating high-quality artistic images for presentations and projects
uniHack: Use descriptive style keywords like 'editorial illustration' or 'flat design infographic' for cleaner academic visuals
description: Midjourney is the leading AI image generation platform known for producing stunning artistic and photorealistic images from text prompts. Students use it to create custom visuals for presentations, posters, and creative projects. The web interface replaced the Discord-only workflow, making it significantly more accessible for everyday use.

name: DALL-E 3
tagline: OpenAI's image generator with precise prompt understanding
bestFor: Generating images that precisely match complex text descriptions
uniHack: Ask ChatGPT to help you craft a detailed DALL-E prompt to get exactly the visual you need for your poster
description: DALL-E 3 is OpenAI's image generation model built into ChatGPT that excels at understanding nuanced, complex prompts and generating images that closely match the description. It can render legible text within images, making it useful for creating infographics, posters, and slides. Access through ChatGPT Plus includes up to 50 image generations per day.

name: Adobe Firefly
tagline: AI image generator built for commercial safe, responsible creation
bestFor: Creating images safe for academic and commercial use
uniHack: Use Firefly inside Photoshop via Generative Fill to add or remove elements from existing project photos
description: Adobe Firefly is trained on licensed and public domain content, making it safe for academic submissions and commercial student projects without copyright concerns. It integrates with Photoshop, Illustrator, and Express for a seamless creative workflow. Students with an Adobe Creative Cloud subscription often get Firefly credits included.

name: Stable Diffusion
tagline: Open-source AI image generator you can run locally
bestFor: Students who want full control and privacy for image generation
uniHack: Run it locally on your campus GPU cluster for free unlimited generations without usage limits
description: Stable Diffusion is the leading open-source text-to-image model that students can run locally on their own hardware for unlimited image generation without usage limits. Its vibrant community has created thousands of fine-tuned models for specific styles and subjects. Local use ensures privacy and no per-image costs.

name: Leonardo AI
tagline: AI image platform with fine-tuned models for creative projects
bestFor: Creating consistent character and game-asset visuals
uniHack: Use the Canvas editor to iteratively edit generated images for consistent visual style across a project
description: Leonardo AI offers a wide range of fine-tuned image generation models suited for game design, concept art, and creative projects. The platform provides detailed control over style, aspect ratio, and character consistency across multiple generations. Its generous daily free token allowance makes it accessible to students on a budget.

name: Sora
tagline: OpenAI's text-to-video generator for cinematic AI videos
bestFor: Creating short cinematic video clips for creative student projects
uniHack: Describe your desired scene shot by shot in detailed terms to get the closest result to your vision
description: Sora by OpenAI generates highly realistic short videos from text prompts, enabling students to create cinematic clips for film projects, presentations, and creative assignments. Its storyboard feature lets users plan multi-scene sequences. It is accessible through a ChatGPT Plus subscription with a monthly generation limit.

name: Runway Gen-3
tagline: Professional AI video generation and editing platform
bestFor: AI video effects, editing, and generation for media students
uniHack: Use the image-to-video feature to animate sketches or concept art for your film or design portfolio
description: Runway offers Gen-3 Alpha, one of the most capable AI video generation models available, alongside professional editing tools for background removal, inpainting, and motion tracking. Media and film students use it to add AI-generated sequences to their productions. The platform supports both text-to-video and image-to-video workflows.

name: Pika
tagline: Easy AI video generation and animation from text or images
bestFor: Quickly animating images and concepts for class presentations
uniHack: Use Pika to animate a key diagram from your research paper to make your conference presentation stand out
description: Pika Labs offers an accessible AI video generation tool that lets students turn text descriptions or static images into short animated clips. Its lip sync feature can animate a character speaking along to audio, useful for storytelling and creative media projects. The simple interface makes it approachable for non-technical creative students.

name: HeyGen
tagline: Create AI video avatars and translate videos to any language
bestFor: Creating presenter videos without camera setup
uniHack: Use a photo avatar to record video project presentations without needing a proper camera setup
description: HeyGen lets students create professional-looking presenter videos using a realistic AI avatar generated from a single photo. Its video translation feature can dub any video into over 40 languages with synced lip movements, making it useful for international student projects. The platform significantly reduces the production burden for required video assignments.

name: Synthesia
tagline: AI video creation with 230+ avatar presenters
bestFor: Making professional explainer videos for class projects
uniHack: Write a script in your notes app and paste it directly into Synthesia to generate a polished explainer video
description: Synthesia turns text scripts into professional presenter videos using realistic AI avatars, requiring no camera, actors, or recording equipment. Students can create polished explainer videos for any subject in over 140 languages. It is widely used in education for creating multilingual instructional content.

name: Beautiful.ai
tagline: AI presentation tool that designs slides automatically as you type
bestFor: Making professional slides without design skills
uniHack: Choose a template with your topic keywords and let the AI suggest layout variants that suit your content structure
description: Beautiful.ai uses smart templates that automatically adjust layouts, font sizes, and spacing as students add content, eliminating manual design work. The result is consistently professional-looking slides without requiring design expertise. Its library of subject-specific templates covers common academic presentation formats.

name: Gamma
tagline: AI-generated presentations, documents, and webpages from a prompt
bestFor: Generating a full presentation draft from a one-line prompt
uniHack: Type your assignment topic and let Gamma draft a full outline with slides in under 60 seconds
description: Gamma allows students to generate complete presentations, documents, or webpages from a single text prompt or outline in seconds. Its AI designs the entire deck including layouts, icons, and formatting without any manual slide-building. The resulting presentations can be shared as interactive web links rather than static files.

name: Tome
tagline: AI storytelling and presentation tool with dynamic layouts
bestFor: Creating visually engaging narrative presentations
uniHack: Use the narrative outline builder to organize your thesis argument visually before writing it out
description: Tome is an AI presentation tool focused on visual storytelling with flexible tile-based layouts that adapt fluidly to different content types. It generates presentation content from prompts and can embed AI-generated images, videos, and live data. Students find it particularly useful for narrative-driven academic presentations.

name: Canva AI
tagline: All-in-one design platform with powerful AI generation features
pricingDetail: Free forever; Pro $15/month; free for students via Education program
bestFor: Creating posters, infographics, resumes, and presentations
uniHack: Apply for Canva for Education to get the Pro plan free if your school email is eligible
description: Canva is the most popular design platform among students, offering AI tools like Magic Design, background remover, and text-to-image generation inside a drag-and-drop interface. It covers every design need from academic posters and infographics to resumes and club banners. The Canva for Education program grants Pro access free to verified students.

name: Adobe Express
tagline: Quick-start design tool with AI features for students and creators
pricingDetail: Free plan; Premium $9.99/month; included with Creative Cloud
bestFor: Quick social media graphics, flyers, and academic posters
uniHack: Use the free student discount on Creative Cloud to unlock Express Premium along with Photoshop and Illustrator
description: Adobe Express is a streamlined design tool ideal for students creating quick visual content like social media posts, flyers, and academic posters. Its AI features include generative text effects, instant background removal, and one-click image enhancement. Students with Creative Cloud subscriptions get Premium access included.

name: Figma AI
tagline: Collaborative UI design tool with built-in AI features
pricingDetail: Free Starter; Education plan free for students
bestFor: Designing app interfaces and prototypes for CS and design courses
uniHack: Apply for the Figma Education plan for free Professional access through your university email
description: Figma is the industry-standard collaborative design tool for UI/UX, used in design courses worldwide. Its AI features can generate UI components from text prompts and auto-organize messy design files. The Education plan grants full Professional access to students verified through their university email.

name: Julius AI
tagline: AI data analyst that interprets and visualizes your datasets with chat
bestFor: Analyzing research datasets without knowing data science
uniHack: Upload your survey CSV and ask Julius to find statistically significant patterns for your research paper
description: Julius AI lets students upload spreadsheets and datasets and ask questions about them in plain English, generating charts, statistical summaries, and insights automatically. It generates the underlying Python code for each analysis so students can learn as they use it. Particularly valuable for social science and business students handling survey data.

name: Noteable
tagline: AI-powered collaborative notebooks for data science and analysis
bestFor: Collaborative data analysis projects in Jupyter-style notebooks
uniHack: Use the AI cell assistant to get code suggestions for the next analysis step directly in your notebook
description: Noteable provides cloud-based collaborative data science notebooks with an AI assistant that suggests the next analysis step, generates visualizations, and explains output. Students can work on data assignments together in real time without local Python setup. It supports Python, R, and SQL notebooks.

name: Gemini
tagline: Google's multimodal AI assistant for writing, research, and coding
bestFor: Research integrated with Google Workspace and real-time web access
uniHack: Connect Gemini to your Google Drive to analyze and summarize files directly from your student folder
description: Gemini is Google's flagship AI assistant deeply integrated with Google Docs, Sheets, Gmail, and other Workspace tools that most students already use. It provides real-time web access for up-to-date research and can analyze uploaded images, documents, and data. The Advanced tier unlocks the most capable Gemini Ultra model for complex tasks.

name: Microsoft Copilot
tagline: AI assistant built into Microsoft 365 for documents, email, and more
pricingDetail: Free consumer version; Microsoft 365 Copilot via university license
bestFor: Writing and editing within Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
uniHack: Check if your university Microsoft 365 license includes Copilot for free access inside Word and Teams
description: Microsoft Copilot is an AI assistant embedded throughout the Microsoft 365 suite, helping students draft Word documents, analyze Excel data, build PowerPoint decks, and summarize Teams meeting notes. Many universities provide Microsoft 365 licenses that may include Copilot access at no extra cost. The consumer version on the web is free and uses GPT-4.

name: Obsidian AI
tagline: Personal knowledge management with AI-powered note linking
bestFor: Building a personal research database that connects ideas
uniHack: Install the Copilot for Obsidian plugin to chat with your entire vault of lecture notes
description: Obsidian is a powerful note-taking app that stores notes as local Markdown files and uses bidirectional linking to build a personal knowledge graph. Students use it to connect ideas across courses, creating a permanent research database. Community AI plugins like Copilot for Obsidian enable chatting with an entire vault of notes.

name: Speechify
tagline: AI text-to-speech tool for reading any content aloud at speed
pricingDetail: Free basic; Premium $139/year; student deals available
bestFor: Consuming textbooks and papers faster through audio
uniHack: Listen to your textbook chapters at 2x speed while commuting to cover more material per day
description: Speechify converts any text, PDF, webpage, or ebook into natural-sounding audio that students can listen to at up to 4.5 times normal speed. It is particularly valuable for students with dyslexia or those who absorb information better through audio. The Chrome extension reads any webpage aloud, including Canvas assignment descriptions.

name: Mindgrasp AI
tagline: AI that generates notes, flashcards, and quizzes from any content
bestFor: Converting lectures, videos, and PDFs into study materials automatically
uniHack: Upload a lecture recording and get auto-generated notes and a quiz ready for exam review in minutes
description: Mindgrasp takes uploaded documents, videos, podcasts, and lecture recordings and automatically generates structured notes, flashcards, and quizzes from the content. Students can process a full semester's worth of materials quickly and review them systematically. It supports a wide range of file formats including YouTube links.

name: Scite.ai
tagline: AI tool showing how papers are cited: supporting, contrasting, or mentioning
pricingDetail: Free limited; Individual $20/month; often free via university library
bestFor: Understanding how a paper's claims are supported or disputed
uniHack: Check if your university library provides free Scite access before buying a subscription
description: Scite displays how academic papers are cited by others, classifying each citation as supporting, contrasting, or merely mentioning the original claim. This helps students quickly assess whether a paper's findings are well-supported or contested in the field. Many university libraries provide institutional access at no cost to enrolled students.

name: Zotero
tagline: Free open-source reference manager with AI features for citations
bestFor: Managing research citations and generating bibliographies
uniHack: Install the browser extension to save any paper or webpage to your library with one click
description: Zotero is the gold-standard free reference manager for academics and students, capable of saving papers from any website and automatically generating formatted bibliographies in APA, MLA, Chicago, or any other style. Its browser extension captures paper metadata with one click. Collaborative shared libraries make group research projects much easier to manage.

name: Napkin AI
tagline: Turn text into visual diagrams and infographics automatically
bestFor: Converting written explanations into clean visual diagrams
uniHack: Paste your essay paragraph about a complex process and get a visual diagram ready for your presentation
description: Napkin AI converts written content into clean visual diagrams, flowcharts, and infographics automatically without any design work. Students can paste a paragraph explaining a concept and receive a presentation-ready visual in seconds. It supports multiple diagram styles and exports as SVG or PNG for use in slides or papers.

name: Miro AI
tagline: Collaborative whiteboard with AI-powered mind mapping and ideation
bestFor: Group brainstorming, mind mapping, and project planning
uniHack: Use the free Education plan via your university email to get unlimited boards for group projects
description: Miro is a leading online collaborative whiteboard used by student teams for brainstorming, project planning, and mind mapping. Its AI features can auto-generate mind maps from a topic keyword and cluster related sticky notes into themes. The Education plan through university emails offers unlimited boards for free.

name: Lumen5
tagline: Turn blog posts and text into social-ready videos with AI
bestFor: Turning written content into explainer videos for class projects
uniHack: Paste your assignment summary to create a short explainer video for class without filming anything
description: Lumen5 automatically converts blog articles or written scripts into formatted video slideshows using AI to match text with relevant stock footage and visuals. Students use it to create quick explainer videos from assignment notes without any video editing skills. The free plan produces watermarked videos suitable for practice projects.

name: Descript
tagline: Edit video and podcast by editing the text transcript like a document
bestFor: Editing recorded lectures and video projects without timeline editing
uniHack: Record your group presentation, then use Descript to remove filler words like 'um' in one click
description: Descript lets students edit video recordings simply by editing the auto-generated transcript, making it the fastest way to clean up recorded lectures, presentations, or podcasts. It automatically removes filler words and silences with one click. The screen recorder and teleprompter make it a complete production tool for student media projects.

name: DeepL
tagline: Highest-accuracy AI translation tool for academic content
bestFor: Translating academic papers, notes, and assignments accurately
uniHack: Use DeepL to read research papers originally published in German, French, or Japanese
description: DeepL is widely regarded as the most accurate AI translation tool available, producing nuanced translations that preserve academic tone better than most alternatives. International students use it to work with papers and references in other languages. The document translation feature processes entire PDFs and preserves formatting.

name: Hemingway Editor
tagline: Highlights complex sentences to help you write more clearly
pricingDetail: Free web editor; Desktop app $19.99 one-time
bestFor: Simplifying academic writing for clearer communication
uniHack: Run your essay through Hemingway before submission to eliminate passive voice and overly complex sentences
description: The Hemingway Editor highlights complex sentences, passive voice, adverbs, and hard-to-read passages in different colors, guiding students to write more clearly and concisely. It provides a readability grade level for the text and simpler word suggestions. Particularly useful for students writing across disciplines where clear communication matters.

name: Lex
tagline: AI-powered word processor designed for distraction-free writing
bestFor: Focused long-form writing like essays and research reports
uniHack: Use the ++ shortcut to instantly get AI continuation when you are stuck on a paragraph mid-essay
description: Lex is a minimalist AI word processor designed for long-form writing where students can press a hotkey to get AI to continue their draft when they get stuck. Its clean distraction-free interface helps with focused essay and report writing sessions. The feedback feature lets AI provide editorial suggestions on a completed draft.

name: Lmstudio
tagline: Run open-source AI models locally on your laptop
bestFor: Running AI models locally for privacy-sensitive student projects
uniHack: Download a small quantized model like Llama 3 to run AI privately on your laptop without any API costs
description: LM Studio is a free desktop application that lets students download and run open-source AI models like Llama and Mistral locally on their own computer without internet or API costs. It provides a clean chat interface and an OpenAI-compatible local API for building privacy-safe applications. Ideal for CS students building AI projects where data privacy is a concern.

name: Ollama
tagline: Command-line tool to run open-source LLMs locally with one command
bestFor: Running AI models locally for dev projects via terminal
uniHack: Run 'ollama run llama3' in your terminal to get a free local chatbot for any coding or study task
description: Ollama is an open-source tool that lets students run open-source language models locally with a single terminal command. It supports over 100 models including Llama, Mistral, and Gemma and exposes a REST API compatible with OpenAI libraries. It is completely free and requires no account, making it ideal for CS students and researchers.

name: Hugging Face
tagline: Open-source AI model hub and collaborative ML platform
bestFor: Accessing, fine-tuning, and deploying open-source AI models
uniHack: Use free Spaces to host your ML course project with a Gradio demo accessible to your professor
description: Hugging Face is the central hub for open-source AI models, datasets, and machine learning tools used by students and researchers worldwide. Students can find pre-trained models for NLP, computer vision, and audio tasks and deploy interactive demos using free Spaces. It is a core part of any ML course curriculum.

name: Coggle
tagline: Collaborative AI-assisted mind mapping for study and brainstorming
bestFor: Visually organizing study topics and essay structures
uniHack: Map out your entire essay argument structure in Coggle before writing to avoid losing your thread
description: Coggle is a simple, collaborative mind mapping tool that lets students visually organize ideas, essay outlines, and study topics on an infinite canvas. Multiple students can edit the same map simultaneously for collaborative brainstorming sessions. The clean interface makes it easy to restructure complex information hierarchically.

name: Mem.ai
tagline: AI note-taking app that automatically organizes your knowledge
bestFor: Automatically organizing class notes without manual filing
uniHack: Dump all your class notes without folders and use Mem Chat to ask questions about any lecture topic
description: Mem.ai is a note-taking app that uses AI to automatically organize notes without folders or tags, finding related content and surfacing it contextually. Students can dump raw lecture notes and later ask the AI to find anything across all their notes in conversational chat. Its self-organizing approach reduces the overhead of maintaining a structured notes system.

name: Gradescope
tagline: AI-assisted assignment and exam grading platform for students and instructors
pricingDetail: Free for students; instructors pay via institution
bestFor: Submitting assignments and getting AI-graded feedback
uniHack: Review the rubric visible in Gradescope before submitting to make sure you hit every requirement
description: Gradescope is an AI-assisted grading platform widely adopted by universities that lets students submit homework and exams digitally and receive structured rubric-based feedback. Its AI groups similar student answers together to help instructors grade consistently. Students benefit from detailed feedback and easy regrade request submissions.

name: Poe
tagline: Access multiple AI models including GPT-4, Claude, and Llama in one app
bestFor: Comparing responses from different AI models in one place
uniHack: Use the multi-bot feature to ask two different AI models the same research question and compare their answers
description: Poe aggregates access to multiple leading AI models including GPT-4, Claude, Llama, and Gemini in a single interface, letting students choose the best model for each task. It supports custom bot creation for specific use cases like essay editing or code review. The free tier provides limited daily access to most major models.

name: Scholarcy
tagline: AI flashcard and summary generator from research papers
bestFor: Extracting key findings and study cards from academic papers
uniHack: Use Scholarcy to auto-extract the key findings table from any paper to quickly assess its relevance
description: Scholarcy reads academic papers and automatically generates structured summaries, flashcards, and key findings tables. Students use it to quickly decide whether a paper is relevant before investing time in reading it fully. It also extracts and hyperlinks all references, making it easy to trace the citation chain back to primary sources.

name: Bing Image Creator
tagline: Free AI image generator powered by DALL-E 3
bestFor: Free high-quality image generation for student projects
uniHack: Use your university Microsoft account to get free DALL-E 3 image generation without a ChatGPT Plus subscription
description: Bing Image Creator provides free access to DALL-E 3 image generation through a Microsoft account, making it the easiest way for students to access high-quality AI image generation without paying for ChatGPT Plus. Students with university Microsoft accounts can use it immediately. It generates four image variations per prompt for download.

name: Ideogram
tagline: AI image generator with excellent text rendering capabilities
bestFor: Creating images with legible text like posters and banners
uniHack: Use Ideogram for event posters where other generators would misspell or distort the text
description: Ideogram AI stands out for its ability to render legible text accurately within generated images, which most other AI image generators struggle with. Students can use it to create event posters, banners, and graphics that include correctly spelled words. The free tier provides 10 generations per day with access to core features.

name: Gamma AI for Education
tagline: Create educational slides and visual learning materials with AI
bestFor: Turning study notes into structured visual study guides
uniHack: Convert your bullet-point notes into a slide deck study guide to review visually before an exam
description: Gamma's AI presentation builder lets students turn rough bullet-point notes into beautifully designed slide decks or study guides. The AI designs layout and typography automatically so students can focus entirely on content. Decks are shared as web links and include analytics showing who viewed the presentation.

name: Prezi
tagline: Dynamic zooming presentations that stand out from standard slides
pricingDetail: Free public presentations; Plus $7/month for students
bestFor: Creating non-linear, engaging presentations for class
uniHack: Use a Prezi map layout to present your argument structure non-linearly and let Q&A guide which branches you explore
description: Prezi uses a unique zoomable canvas instead of linear slides, creating presentations that can navigate non-linearly based on audience questions and interest. Its AI suggests content structure and topics from an outline. Student plans are discounted significantly, and free public presentations are always available.

name: Slides AI
tagline: Generate Google Slides presentations from text with one click
bestFor: Generating Google Slides directly from notes or outlines
uniHack: Paste your assignment notes into SlidesAI to instantly generate a Google Slides deck you can refine
description: SlidesAI is a Google Workspace add-on that generates full Google Slides presentations from pasted text or outlines in one click. Students can paste their lecture notes or essay outline and receive a structured, themed presentation ready to edit. Because it outputs directly to Google Slides, it fits naturally into existing student workflows.

name: Panopto
tagline: AI-powered lecture capture and video management for universities
bestFor: Reviewing recorded lectures with AI-searchable transcripts
uniHack: Use the AI transcript search to jump directly to the part of the lecture where a specific term was mentioned
description: Panopto is the leading university lecture capture platform that automatically transcribes recorded classes and makes every spoken word searchable. Students can type a term and jump instantly to the exact moment in the video where it was discussed. Most universities provide Panopto access free to enrolled students.

name: Tableau Public
tagline: Free data visualization platform for creating interactive charts
pricingDetail: Free for public work; Tableau Desktop via university program
bestFor: Creating compelling data visualizations for research and presentations
uniHack: Apply for Tableau for Students to get the full Desktop version free through your academic email
description: Tableau Public is a free version of Tableau that lets students create professional interactive data visualizations and publish them online. Students in data science, business, and social science programs use it for research presentations and capstone projects. The Tableau for Students program provides the full Tableau Desktop version free with a university email.

name: Orange Data Mining
tagline: Visual drag-and-drop machine learning and data analysis tool
bestFor: Learning machine learning concepts visually without coding
uniHack: Build a complete classification pipeline by dragging widgets together without writing a single line of code
description: Orange is a free open-source data mining tool developed at the University of Ljubljana that uses a visual drag-and-drop interface for machine learning and data analysis. Students can build complete ML pipelines, visualize datasets, and compare algorithms without writing code. It is widely used in university courses to introduce data science concepts.

name: KNIME
tagline: Open-source data analytics platform with visual workflow builder
pricingDetail: Free open source; KNIME Hub for team features
bestFor: Data processing pipelines and analytics projects without coding
uniHack: Use KNIME's free learning courses at university to get hands-on data analytics practice before internships
description: KNIME is a free open-source analytics platform used in universities and enterprises that lets students build data processing and machine learning workflows visually. It integrates with Python and R for advanced scripting when needed. The KNIME learning portal provides free courses that count toward data analytics certifications.

name: Power BI
tagline: Business intelligence tool for creating dashboards and data reports
pricingDetail: Free Desktop app; Pro $10/month, often included in university Microsoft license
bestFor: Building interactive business data dashboards for business courses
uniHack: Check if your university Microsoft 365 license includes Power BI Pro for free report sharing
description: Microsoft Power BI is the leading business intelligence tool for creating interactive dashboards and reports from data sources. Business, accounting, and economics students use it extensively for case studies and capstone projects. The free desktop app provides full report creation, and many universities include Pro sharing through their Microsoft 365 licenses.

name: Google Colab
tagline: Free Jupyter notebooks in the cloud with GPU access for ML projects
bestFor: Running machine learning experiments and Python projects in the cloud
uniHack: Run deep learning models on free GPU hours in Colab instead of waiting for university cluster access
description: Google Colab provides free cloud-hosted Jupyter notebooks with access to NVIDIA GPU and TPU resources, making it the go-to platform for student machine learning projects without expensive local hardware. Notebooks save directly to Google Drive and can be shared instantly. The Pro plan provides better GPUs and longer runtime sessions.

name: Kaggle
tagline: Data science competitions, datasets, and free cloud notebooks
bestFor: Practicing data science with real datasets and community notebooks
uniHack: Complete a Kaggle Getting Started competition and list it on your resume as real data science experience
description: Kaggle is a data science competition platform and community that offers free cloud notebooks with GPU access, thousands of public datasets, and community learning resources. Students participate in competitions to build portfolios and learn from shared winning solutions. It is one of the best free resources for developing practical data science skills.

name: Phind
tagline: AI search engine optimized for programmers and technical questions
bestFor: Finding coding solutions with context-aware technical answers
uniHack: Use Phind instead of Google for programming assignment errors to get targeted code examples with explanation
description: Phind is an AI search engine built for developers that searches the web and returns technically accurate answers with code examples. It understands programming context better than general search engines and cites the exact Stack Overflow or documentation page for each answer. Students find it far more efficient than generic search for debugging and assignment help.

name: CodePen
tagline: Browser-based front-end code playground with AI assistance
bestFor: Prototyping HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for web development courses
uniHack: Share a public CodePen link with your professor or peers for instant feedback on front-end assignments
description: CodePen is a browser-based front-end development environment where students can write HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with a live preview updating in real time. Its AI assistant can generate layouts or fix bugs from a prompt. The vast community library of pens serves as an educational resource for learning modern web development techniques.

name: Glean
tagline: AI workplace search across all university apps and documents
pricingDetail: Enterprise pricing; often provided by university IT
bestFor: Searching across all university platforms and documents from one place
uniHack: Ask if your university has Glean deployed to search Canvas, Google Drive, and email simultaneously
description: Glean is an enterprise AI search platform that connects to all the apps and tools in an organization, enabling unified search across Slack, email, Google Drive, and course platforms. Some universities deploy Glean so students can find any document or conversation across all institutional tools from one search box. Its AI provides summarized answers rather than just links.

name: ElevenLabs
tagline: Hyper-realistic AI voice generation and text-to-speech
bestFor: Creating audio summaries of study notes for audio learners
uniHack: Convert your summarized lecture notes into a realistic voice audio file to listen to while exercising
description: ElevenLabs produces the most natural-sounding text-to-speech audio available, with a library of hundreds of voices and support for 29 languages. Students can paste study notes to create audio files for listening during commutes or workouts. The voice cloning feature allows creating a custom AI voice matching the student's own voice.

name: Fireflies.ai
tagline: AI meeting transcription and summary tool for Zoom and Meet
bestFor: Automatically recording and summarizing study group meetings
uniHack: Add Fireflies as a bot to your study group's Zoom call to get a searchable transcript and action items automatically
description: Fireflies.ai joins video calls as a bot and automatically transcribes, summarizes, and extracts action items from meetings. Study groups use it so no one has to take notes manually and everyone receives a structured summary afterward. The search feature lets students find specific discussions across all past meeting recordings.

name: Taskade
tagline: AI-powered project management and team collaboration workspace
bestFor: Managing group projects with AI-generated tasks and timelines
uniHack: Describe your group project goal and let Taskade AI generate a full breakdown of tasks and deadlines
description: Taskade combines AI task generation with project management tools including lists, mind maps, and Gantt charts in one workspace. Students can describe a project goal and receive a full task breakdown with suggested timelines from the AI. Real-time collaboration makes it ideal for group assignments and research projects.

name: Readwise Reader
tagline: Read-it-later app with AI summaries and highlight review
bestFor: Saving and reviewing academic articles with AI-generated summaries
uniHack: Save all assigned readings to Readwise and review AI-generated highlights before each class
description: Readwise Reader is a read-it-later app that centralizes all academic reading materials including articles, PDFs, and newsletters in one place with AI-generated summaries and smart highlighting. Students can review their highlights from all saved articles using a spaced repetition system to embed key ideas. Integration with Anki and Obsidian deepens the learning workflow.

name: Explain Like I'm Five
tagline: AI that explains complex academic topics in simple, accessible terms
bestFor: Understanding difficult concepts through simple analogies
uniHack: Use it when textbook definitions fail you — get a simple analogy before diving back into the technical definition
description: Explain Like I'm Five uses AI to provide plain-language explanations of complex academic and technical topics at adjustable comprehension levels. When students are stuck on a difficult concept, a simple analogy often unlocks understanding better than a technical definition. The tool is free and requires no account to use.

name: Coursera
tagline: Online courses from top universities with AI-assisted learning paths
pricingDetail: Free audit; certificates from $49; Coursera Plus $59/month
bestFor: Learning new skills with structured university-level courses
uniHack: Audit courses for free to learn supplementary skills relevant to your major without spending anything
description: Coursera hosts thousands of university-level courses from institutions like Stanford, MIT, and Google covering every academic and professional discipline. Students can audit most courses for free to supplement their degree. Its AI recommends learning paths based on career goals and tracks progress with personalized pacing suggestions.

name: Duolingo
tagline: AI-powered language learning with gamified lessons and speaking practice
bestFor: Learning a new language or fulfilling foreign language requirements
uniHack: Use Duolingo alongside your university language course to get extra practice between class sessions
description: Duolingo uses AI to personalize language learning paths and adapt lesson difficulty to each student's progress. Its conversational AI feature allows students to practice speaking with a patient digital partner. With 40+ languages available, it is the most-used supplement for university foreign language requirements.

name: Brainscape
tagline: Adaptive flashcard platform that optimizes review frequency by confidence
bestFor: Adaptive spaced repetition for professional exam prep
uniHack: Rate each card 1-5 honestly so the algorithm knows exactly which concepts need more repetition
description: Brainscape uses a confidence-based repetition system where students rate how well they know each flashcard from 1 to 5, and the algorithm schedules reviews accordingly. It offers certified decks for major professional exams including MCAT, USMLE, bar exams, and language tests. Students preparing for standardized exams use it alongside Anki for comprehensive coverage.

name: Excalidraw
tagline: Free collaborative virtual whiteboard for sketching diagrams and ideas
pricingDetail: Free and open source; Plus $7/month for extra features
bestFor: Quickly sketching architecture diagrams and concept maps with a team
uniHack: Share an Excalidraw link with your study group to sketch solutions together in real time without any login
description: Excalidraw is a free open-source virtual whiteboard with a distinctive hand-drawn aesthetic, used by students for sketching architecture diagrams, flowcharts, and brainstorming layouts. No account is required and rooms can be shared instantly via link for real-time collaborative drawing. It exports clean PNG and SVG files suitable for presentations.

name: Caktus AI
tagline: AI assistant designed specifically for student academic tasks
bestFor: Getting help with a wide range of student assignments and essays
uniHack: Use it to brainstorm essay angles rather than generating content directly to keep your work original
description: Caktus AI is designed specifically for students and offers help with essays, coding, math, and citations in one focused platform. It provides templates for common academic writing formats and includes a citation generator. The tool positions itself as a study aid rather than an essay writing service, though students should use AI responsibly in line with academic policies.

name: Copy.ai
tagline: AI writing assistant for marketing copy, emails, and content
bestFor: Writing persuasive content, emails, and social media for class projects
uniHack: Use Copy.ai to draft outreach emails to professors, internship coordinators, or research supervisors
description: Copy.ai helps students write persuasive emails, social media posts, blog articles, and marketing materials for business and communication courses. Its template library covers dozens of common writing formats, making it easy to start any content task. The free tier provides enough words per month for most casual student needs.

name: Writesonic
tagline: AI writing platform for articles, ads, and academic content
bestFor: Drafting longer articles and research-backed content for journalism courses
uniHack: Use the Chatsonic real-time search feature to draft articles on current events with up-to-date facts
description: Writesonic is an AI writing tool with a Chatsonic feature that searches the web in real time to inform article drafts with current information. Students in journalism, communications, and marketing courses use it to draft research-backed content efficiently. Its article rewriter helps students rephrase sources without plagiarizing.

name: Jasper AI
tagline: Enterprise-grade AI writing assistant for content creation
bestFor: Learning professional content marketing writing for marketing students
uniHack: Use the free trial period to complete your marketing course project before the subscription kicks in
description: Jasper AI is a professional content creation platform popular in the marketing industry that marketing and communications students use to learn enterprise content workflows. It offers templates for blog posts, ad copy, and email campaigns with brand voice customization. Students can complete course projects during the free trial period.

name: Lottiefiles
tagline: Platform for lightweight AI-assisted animations for web and app projects
bestFor: Adding animations to student web and app development projects
uniHack: Find free Lottie animations to add polished micro-interactions to your web development course project
description: LottieFiles hosts a massive library of lightweight, scalable animations in the Lottie format that web and app development students can embed in their projects with minimal code. Its AI can generate custom animations from text descriptions. The free library provides thousands of animations suitable for polishing student projects.

name: Spline
tagline: Browser-based 3D design tool with AI for web and interactive projects
bestFor: Creating interactive 3D visuals for web design and portfolio projects
uniHack: Embed a Spline 3D scene in your portfolio website to instantly stand out from other applicants
description: Spline is a browser-based 3D design tool that lets design students create interactive 3D scenes, animations, and objects without needing specialized 3D software knowledge. Its AI can generate 3D shapes from text descriptions. Completed scenes can be embedded directly into websites as interactive elements, perfect for design portfolio projects.

name: Framer AI
tagline: AI-powered website builder for designing and publishing instantly
bestFor: Building a professional portfolio or project website with no coding
uniHack: Use the free tier to build your student portfolio site and showcase your projects with a custom domain later
description: Framer lets students design and publish professional websites using an AI that generates full page layouts from text descriptions. Its drag-and-drop editor requires no coding while producing code-quality responsive designs. Students commonly use it to build portfolio sites, project showcases, and club pages with a free subdomain.

name: Webflow
tagline: Professional no-code web design platform with CMS and hosting
pricingDetail: Free with subdomain; Lite $16/month; free for students via program
bestFor: Building professional websites and portfolios for design courses
uniHack: Apply for the Webflow for Schools program to get free access for your design or CS coursework
description: Webflow is a professional no-code web design platform that generates clean production-quality HTML and CSS, bridging the gap between design and development. Design students use it for portfolio sites and client projects without writing code. The Webflow for Schools program provides free access to eligible university design programs.

name: Lobe Chat
tagline: Open-source self-hosted AI chat client supporting multiple models
pricingDetail: Free and open source; bring your own API keys
bestFor: Self-hosting a private multi-model AI assistant for CS students
uniHack: Deploy Lobe Chat on your university's free student cloud credits for a private AI assistant with your own API keys
description: Lobe Chat is an open-source AI chat client that can be self-hosted and connected to multiple AI models via API keys, including GPT-4, Claude, and local models. CS students use it to learn about AI API integration while building their own private assistant. It supports a plugin ecosystem that extends functionality to web search, code execution, and more.

name: Cram
tagline: AI flashcard creator with millions of community study sets
bestFor: Finding pre-made flashcard sets for almost any class topic
uniHack: Search your exact textbook or professor name to find flashcard sets made by students in previous semesters
description: Cram.com hosts millions of student-created flashcard sets covering virtually every subject, textbook, and course. Its AI card generation tool can create new sets from pasted text in seconds. Students often find sets created by peers from the same university and textbook, saving significant preparation time before exams.

name: Prism
tagline: Statistical analysis and graphing tool for biomedical research
bestFor: Statistical analysis for biology, medicine, and social science research
uniHack: Use the Prism student license for lab reports and thesis analysis without needing SPSS or SAS
description: GraphPad Prism is the leading statistical analysis and graphing software used in biomedical research, uniquely guiding students to choose the appropriate statistical test for their data type. Students in biology, medicine, and psychology programs use it for lab reports, thesis research, and clinical data analysis. Its publication-ready graphs meet journal submission standards.

name: SPSS
tagline: Statistical software for social science and behavioral research
bestFor: Quantitative social science research and thesis data analysis
uniHack: Check your university software portal — SPSS is almost universally provided free to enrolled students
description: IBM SPSS Statistics is the dominant statistical software in social science and behavioral research, widely taught in psychology, sociology, and business programs. It offers a point-and-click interface for regression, ANOVA, factor analysis, and other common tests. Almost all universities provide SPSS free to enrolled students through software licensing agreements.

name: Maple
tagline: Symbolic math software for advanced mathematics and engineering
bestFor: Symbolic calculus, linear algebra, and differential equations for STEM
uniHack: Use Maple's step-by-step solution feature to learn the mechanics of proofs before attempting them yourself
description: Maple is a symbolic math system used by engineering, mathematics, and physics students for calculus, linear algebra, differential equations, and proof verification. Its document mode lets students combine equations, calculations, and text in a single report-style file. The step-by-step solver is particularly valuable for understanding proof techniques.

name: MATLAB AI
tagline: Industry-standard engineering and scientific computing platform with AI tools
pricingDetail: Free via most engineering university licenses
bestFor: Signal processing, control systems, and machine learning in engineering courses
uniHack: Use the MATLAB AI Chat Playground to get code suggestions and debugging help without leaving the environment
description: MATLAB is the dominant platform for engineering computation in universities, used across electrical, mechanical, civil, and biomedical engineering programs. Its AI coding assistant helps students debug and generate MATLAB code. Most engineering schools provide free campus-wide licenses covering all toolboxes students need for coursework.

name: Mathway
tagline: Step-by-step solver for algebra through calculus and statistics
bestFor: Checking math homework and understanding step-by-step methods
uniHack: Use it free to verify your final answer, then check your own work step by step before paying for steps
description: Mathway provides immediate answers to math problems from basic algebra through advanced calculus and statistics. Students can type or photograph problems to get instant solutions. The free plan shows final answers, while the subscription reveals detailed step-by-step solutions that help students understand methods for upcoming exams.

name: Gemini Advanced
tagline: Google's most capable AI model with Gemini Ultra and deep Workspace integration
bestFor: Complex research, long document analysis, and deep workspace automation
uniHack: Use the 2M token context window to upload an entire thesis draft and ask Gemini to review it holistically
description: Gemini Advanced provides access to Google's most capable Gemini Ultra model with a 2-million-token context window, enabling analysis of very long documents, entire codebases, or complete research collections. Its deep integration with Google Workspace lets it read and write across Gmail, Docs, Sheets, and Meet. The Google One AI Premium subscription includes both Gemini Advanced and 2TB of storage.

name: Focusmate
tagline: Virtual co-working platform with accountability partners for study sessions
bestFor: Staying focused and accountable during long study sessions
uniHack: Schedule a Focusmate session every morning during exam week to maintain a consistent study routine
description: Focusmate connects students with accountability partners for timed virtual co-working sessions via video call, dramatically improving focus and follow-through on study commitments. Each session starts with stating a goal and ends with a brief check-in on progress. Students report significantly higher productivity during exam periods when using Focusmate daily.

name: Forest
tagline: Gamified focus app that grows virtual trees while you study
bestFor: Building phone-free study habits with gamified accountability
uniHack: Start a study forest for your entire study group to build collective accountability and see who has the biggest forest
description: Forest gamifies focus sessions by growing a virtual tree that dies if you leave the app during a study session, making phone distraction tangible and motivating. Students accumulate a virtual forest tracking all their study hours and can use earned virtual coins to plant real trees. It is one of the most loved student productivity apps globally.

name: Notion Calendar
tagline: AI-connected calendar app that links to your Notion workspace
bestFor: Connecting your Notion study database with a clean calendar view
uniHack: Link your Notion assignment database to the calendar to see all deadlines plotted visually across the semester
description: Notion Calendar is a dedicated calendar app from Notion that connects directly to your Notion databases, letting students link class schedules and assignment deadline databases to a visual calendar. It integrates with Google Calendar for a unified view of all academic and personal commitments. The app is free and syncs in real time with your Notion workspace.

name: Raindrop.io
tagline: AI-enhanced visual bookmark manager for organizing research sources
bestFor: Organizing and tagging research sources, papers, and article links
uniHack: Create a collection per research paper and use AI tagging to find any source instantly across all your projects
description: Raindrop.io is a visual bookmark manager that lets students collect, tag, and search all their research links, articles, and web resources in one place. Its AI auto-tagging feature categorizes saved bookmarks automatically, making it easy to organize sources across multiple research projects. The Pro plan includes full-text search within saved pages.

name: Mural
tagline: Digital whiteboard and visual collaboration tool for design thinking
pricingDetail: Free trial; Team $12/month; Education program available
bestFor: Design thinking workshops, user journey mapping, and group ideation
uniHack: Use the free Mural for Education program for user research and design thinking exercises in UX courses
description: Mural is a visual collaboration platform used in design, MBA, and innovation courses for activities like user journey mapping, retrospectives, and stakeholder analysis. Its AI facilitator can suggest the next exercise based on the group's current activity. The Education program provides free team access to eligible students and instructors.

name: Krisp
tagline: AI background noise cancellation for video calls and recordings
bestFor: Removing background noise from dorm rooms during online classes
uniHack: Enable Krisp before joining online classes from a noisy campus cafe or dorm room to sound professional
description: Krisp uses AI to remove background noise, echoes, and voices from both microphone input and speaker output in real time. Students participating in online classes or recording assignments from noisy environments use it to sound professional. The free tier covers 60 minutes of noise cancellation per day, enough for most lecture sessions.

name: MyMap.AI
tagline: AI mind map and flowchart generator from text prompts
bestFor: Creating mind maps from pasted notes for visual learning
uniHack: Paste your lecture notes into MyMap to get an instant visual overview of all key topics and their relationships
description: MyMap.AI generates structured mind maps and flowcharts from text input or topic keywords using AI, helping students visualize complex information hierarchically. Students paste lecture notes or reading summaries and receive an organized map of all key ideas and relationships. Maps can be exported as images or shared via link.

name: Poe AI
tagline: Access Claude, GPT-4, and Llama in one app with custom bots
bestFor: Accessing multiple AI models and creating custom study bots
uniHack: Build a custom subject-specific bot with a system prompt tuned for your exact course to get more relevant answers
description: Poe aggregates access to multiple AI models in a single interface and lets students create custom bots with tailored system prompts for specific courses or study tasks. A student can build a dedicated chemistry tutor bot with strict prompting that always explains things in terms of their textbook. The bot sharing feature lets classmates benefit from each other's configurations.

name: Luminar AI
tagline: AI-powered photo editing software for students and creators
bestFor: Editing photos for creative projects and photography courses
uniHack: Use the AI Sky Replacement and Portrait tools to make quick professional edits without deep Photoshop knowledge
description: Luminar AI uses artificial intelligence to automate complex photo editing tasks like sky replacement, portrait enhancement, and background removal that would take hours in traditional software. Photography and digital media students use it to achieve professional results with minimal training. The one-time purchase model is more student-friendly than Photoshop subscriptions.

name: Research Rabbit
tagline: AI tool for discovering and visualizing academic paper networks
bestFor: Discovering related papers and building literature maps for research
uniHack: Add your first five key papers to start a collection and let Research Rabbit find everything cited and citing them
description: Research Rabbit is a completely free tool that builds visual maps of academic paper networks, helping students discover related works they would never have found through keyword search. It integrates with Zotero and shows papers that cite and are cited by your saved collection. Students call it a Spotify recommendation engine for academic papers.

name: Gemini in Google Docs
tagline: AI writing assistant built directly into Google Docs for students
pricingDetail: Free via Google Workspace Education accounts
bestFor: Drafting, rewriting, and summarizing within Google Docs
uniHack: Use the Help me write sidebar in Google Docs to draft section outlines directly in your existing assignment document
description: Gemini integrated into Google Docs provides AI writing assistance directly in the document students already use for assignments. It can draft paragraphs, rewrite selected text, summarize long documents, and answer questions about document content. Students with Google Workspace for Education accounts often get this feature free through their university.

name: Penelope AI
tagline: AI writing assistant that helps improve academic writing quality
bestFor: Improving the quality and flow of academic essays and reports
uniHack: Paste your conclusion paragraph to get AI suggestions for stronger academic language and tighter argumentation
description: Penelope AI focuses specifically on improving academic writing quality, helping students elevate informal phrasing to scholarly register, improve argument structure, and polish essay conclusions. Unlike general writing tools, it understands the conventions of academic tone and citation-heavy writing. The free tier covers most occasional use cases for student papers.

name: ClickUp AI
tagline: All-in-one project management with built-in AI writing and task features
bestFor: Managing complex group research projects and assignment pipelines
uniHack: Use ClickUp Docs with AI to write your project plan and link it directly to tasks with assigned deadlines
description: ClickUp is a comprehensive project management platform combining tasks, docs, whiteboards, and chat with an AI assistant that can generate project plans, write task descriptions, and summarize documents. Student teams use it for managing complex research projects and capstone deliverables. The free plan is generous enough for most student team projects.

name: Genially
tagline: Create interactive presentations, infographics, and gamified content
bestFor: Creating interactive and gamified class presentations and infographics
uniHack: Add hidden hotspots to your presentation slides that reveal extra information when classmates click on them
description: Genially enables students to create interactive presentations and infographics with clickable hotspots, embedded videos, animations, and quiz elements that engage audiences far beyond standard slide decks. The platform is popular in education for creating learning experiences as well as presentations. The free plan provides unlimited creations that can be shared publicly.

name: Typeform
tagline: Conversational survey and form builder with AI-generated questions
bestFor: Creating engaging surveys for research and student feedback collection
uniHack: Use AI to generate a complete survey from your research question and get high response rates with the conversational format
description: Typeform creates one-question-at-a-time survey experiences that achieve significantly higher completion rates than traditional forms. Students use it to collect primary research data for dissertations, capstone projects, and market research assignments. Its AI can generate complete survey question sets from a research objective description.

name: Loom AI
tagline: Screen recording with AI transcription, summaries, and chapters
bestFor: Recording project presentations and getting AI-summarized feedback
uniHack: Record your project demo with Loom and share the link instead of scheduling live presentations with professors
description: Loom allows students to instantly record screen and camera videos and share them via link without any file uploads. Its AI automatically generates transcripts, creates chapters from topic shifts, and summarizes the video content. Students submit project demos, coding walkthroughs, and tutorial videos using Loom to professors and teammates.

name: Aria by Opera
tagline: Built-in browser AI assistant for research and browsing
bestFor: Summarizing webpages and researching without leaving the browser
uniHack: Open Aria in the Opera sidebar to summarize any research webpage without opening a separate tab or tool
description: Aria is Opera's built-in browser AI assistant that lives in the sidebar and can summarize any webpage, answer research questions, and generate images without switching to a separate tool. Students who use Opera as their main browser get free built-in AI capabilities without needing any additional subscriptions. The AI is updated regularly with access to new models.

name: Pictory AI
tagline: Turn articles and scripts into videos with AI automatically
bestFor: Creating video presentations from written content for assignments
uniHack: Paste your essay to generate a summary video for social media to build your academic presence online
description: Pictory automatically transforms written scripts, articles, or blog posts into short videos by matching content with relevant stock footage and generating captions. Students use it to create video summaries of their research or to produce video content for communications and journalism courses. The free trial includes three complete project exports.

name: GoodNotes AI
tagline: AI-enhanced digital note-taking and handwriting recognition app
bestFor: Digital handwritten notes with AI-powered search and flashcards
uniHack: Enable AI flashcard generation to automatically create study cards from your handwritten class notes
description: GoodNotes is the leading digital note-taking app for students using iPads, offering natural handwriting experience with Apple Pencil and powerful AI features. Its AI can search handwritten text across all notebooks, auto-generate flashcards from notes, and provide smart math tools for STEM students. The affordable annual plan makes it accessible for most students.

name: Notability
tagline: iPad note-taking app with AI handwriting conversion and audio sync
bestFor: Syncing handwritten notes to lecture audio for efficient review
uniHack: Enable audio recording during lectures so you can tap any note and jump to the exact moment it was spoken
description: Notability allows students to record lectures while taking handwritten notes, creating a synchronized connection between writing and audio. Tapping any written note later jumps playback to the moment it was written, making review sessions highly efficient. Its AI converts handwriting to searchable text and the multi-note view lets students reference multiple classes simultaneously.

name: Detangle.ai
tagline: Simplifies complex legal and policy documents into plain English
bestFor: Understanding academic policies, terms of service, and legal reading assignments
uniHack: Upload your university housing contract or academic integrity policy to understand exactly what you are agreeing to
