description: Detangle AI translates complex legal documents, contracts, and policy texts into plain English summaries. Law students use it to quickly identify key clauses in case law, while all students benefit from understanding the academic policies and agreements they must sign. The PDF upload feature handles most legal document formats.

name: Codeacademy AI
tagline: Interactive coding courses with AI-powered hints and feedback
bestFor: Learning programming from scratch with interactive AI-guided lessons
uniHack: Complete a Codecademy skill path before starting a related CS elective to come in better prepared
description: Codecademy offers interactive programming courses in over 20 languages with an AI assistant that provides context-aware hints when students get stuck without giving away the answer. Its structured career paths guide students from zero to job-ready skills. Free content covers many foundational topics and the Plus subscription unlocks real-world projects.

name: Udio
tagline: AI music generator that creates original songs from text prompts
bestFor: Creating original background music for video and media projects
uniHack: Generate royalty-free background music for your student film project with a specific mood and genre prompt
description: Udio generates full original songs from text prompts specifying genre, mood, instruments, and even lyrics. Media and film students use it to create royalty-free background music for video projects, podcasts, and presentations. The free monthly credits are sufficient for several complete track generations for student projects.

name: Suno
tagline: AI music creation platform that generates songs with vocals from prompts
bestFor: Creating songs with AI vocals for creative and media projects
uniHack: Write custom lyrics and describe the style to generate a unique song for a music theory or media production course
description: Suno AI generates complete songs with AI vocals from text prompts describing genre, mood, and custom lyrics. Music theory students use it to hear their compositional ideas performed and media production students use it for creative soundtracks. The generous daily free credits make it accessible without a subscription for casual student use.

name: Ahrefs AI Writing Tools
tagline: Free AI writing tools including paragraph and essay generators
bestFor: Free AI tools for paragraph writing, paraphrasing, and content outlining
uniHack: Bookmark the free Ahrefs paragraph generator for quick drafts when starting a new essay section feels daunting
description: Ahrefs offers a collection of free AI writing tools including a paragraph generator, essay outline creator, paraphrasing tool, and grammar checker that require no account or subscription. Students use them as quick utilities without needing a full AI writing subscription. Each tool is specialized and fast, ideal for targeted writing assistance.

name: Consensus Summarizer
tagline: Daily summaries of the most important academic papers across fields
bestFor: Staying current with research in your field without reading every paper
uniHack: Subscribe to your major field digest to passively absorb current research trends without extra study time
description: Paper Digest delivers daily email summaries of the most impactful new academic papers across selected research fields. Students subscribe to their discipline's digest to stay current with cutting-edge research effortlessly. Each summary is concise enough to read in under two minutes and includes a link to the full paper for deeper reading.

name: Humata AI
tagline: Chat with any PDF document for instant Q&A and summaries
bestFor: Asking questions directly to uploaded research papers and textbooks
uniHack: Upload the full textbook chapter PDF and ask Humata to explain the sections you do not understand
description: Humata AI allows students to upload PDF documents and ask questions about the content in natural language, getting precise answers with referenced page numbers. The student plan is extremely affordable, making it one of the best value document AI tools. Multi-document analysis enables cross-referencing across multiple papers simultaneously.

name: PDF.ai
tagline: Chat with any PDF instantly for quick answers and summaries
bestFor: Quickly extracting information from dense PDF textbooks and papers
uniHack: Upload your syllabus PDF to quickly find assignment details and grading breakdowns without scanning pages manually
description: PDF.ai lets students upload any PDF and immediately start asking questions about the content in a chat interface. It works with textbooks, research papers, syllabi, and any other PDF academic materials. The tool highlights the exact source passage in the document that corresponds to each answer for easy verification.

name: Study.ai
tagline: Personalized AI study plans and quizzes from your own materials
bestFor: Building personalized study schedules and practice quizzes from your notes
uniHack: Upload all your notes at once and let the AI create a week-by-week study schedule toward your exam date
description: Study.ai generates personalized study schedules and practice quizzes from students' own uploaded notes and materials. It analyzes what students know and what they need to work on, adjusting the study plan accordingly as they practice. The tool integrates spaced repetition principles into automatically generated quiz sessions.

name: Wize
tagline: AI-powered exam prep platform for accounting and professional exams
bestFor: CPA, CFA, and professional exam preparation with AI practice
uniHack: Use the adaptive quiz feature to concentrate your study time on the specific topic areas where you score lowest
description: Wize is a specialized AI exam prep platform focused on accounting and professional certifications including CPA, CFA, and EA exams. Its AI identifies student weaknesses through adaptive quizzing and focuses study time on the lowest-scoring areas. Video explanations from real instructors accompany each topic area for comprehensive preparation.

name: Natural Readers
tagline: Free text-to-speech reader for any text or document online
bestFor: Listening to study materials for students who prefer auditory learning
uniHack: Paste your essay into Natural Readers to proofread it by hearing it aloud — you will catch errors you would never see
description: Natural Readers provides free text-to-speech reading of any text directly in the browser without requiring downloads. Students with dyslexia or those who prefer audio learning use it to consume textbooks, articles, and class readings more efficiently. The Chrome extension reads any webpage and the proofreading use case helps all students catch writing errors.

name: Deepgram
tagline: Fast accurate speech-to-text API for building transcription applications
bestFor: Building speech-to-text features in CS project applications
uniHack: Use the free $200 credit to add voice input features to your capstone project without spending anything
description: Deepgram offers one of the fastest and most accurate speech-to-text APIs, allowing CS students to add voice transcription to their project applications. The free $200 signup credit is enough for extensive development and testing. Its developer-friendly SDK and documentation make integration straightforward for student hackathon and capstone projects.

name: Rows
tagline: Spreadsheet with built-in AI for data analysis without formulas
bestFor: Analyzing and visualizing data using natural language in a spreadsheet
uniHack: Describe in plain English what calculation you need and let the AI write the formula for you instead of guessing
description: Rows is a spreadsheet that lets students describe data analysis tasks in plain language and have AI perform calculations, write formulas, and generate charts automatically. It bridges the gap between traditional spreadsheet skills and data analysis needs, letting students focus on insights rather than formula syntax. Integration with external data sources makes it useful for real research datasets.

name: Veed.io AI
tagline: Browser-based video editor with AI captions, translation, and tools
bestFor: Adding subtitles and cleaning up recorded project videos online
uniHack: Upload your recorded presentation and use Veed's AI to add automatic captions in under 2 minutes
description: Veed.io is a browser-based video editor that makes it simple to add AI-generated captions, remove backgrounds, and translate video content without installing software. Students use it to add accessibility-friendly subtitles to project videos and clean up recordings for class submissions. Its AI eye contact correction feature helps recorded presentations look more natural.

name: Pitch
tagline: Modern team presentation tool with AI design suggestions
bestFor: Collaboratively building and iterating on presentation decks with a team
uniHack: Use Pitch for group presentation projects where multiple students need to edit slides simultaneously
description: Pitch is a team presentation tool that combines beautiful templates, real-time collaborative editing, and AI design suggestions in a professional interface. Student groups use it when multiple people need to build and iterate on a presentation deck simultaneously. Presenter analytics show which slides viewers spent the most time on after sharing.

name: Paperpal
tagline: AI academic writing assistant for grammar, clarity, and research integrity
bestFor: Improving academic writing quality and checking research integrity
uniHack: Run your introduction through Paperpal to elevate your academic register before submitting to a journal
description: Paperpal is trained specifically on academic content and provides writing suggestions tuned to scholarly norms rather than general English. It integrates directly with Microsoft Word and the browser for seamless editing and includes plagiarism detection. Students preparing journal submissions or thesis chapters particularly benefit from its academic-register suggestions.

name: AI Dungeon
tagline: AI-powered text adventure game for creative writing and storytelling
bestFor: Developing creative writing and narrative skills through interactive storytelling
uniHack: Use it as a creative writing sandbox to practice character development and plot structuring for fiction writing courses
description: AI Dungeon is an interactive narrative game where players co-write unlimited collaborative stories with an AI that responds to any action or direction. Creative writing students use it to practice storytelling, character development, and world-building in an interactive sandbox. It is also useful for overcoming writer's block by exploring narrative possibilities freely.

name: Character.AI
tagline: Chat with AI characters including historical figures for interactive learning
bestFor: Practicing language conversations and interviewing historical characters
uniHack: Chat with a Socratic philosopher bot to practice philosophy dialogue or with a native speaker bot for language practice
description: Character.AI lets students interact with AI characters including historical figures, fictional personas, and custom-built educational characters. Language students use native speaker characters for conversational practice. History and philosophy students engage historical figures in Socratic dialogue for a unique active learning experience.

name: Claude for Sheets
tagline: Use Claude AI directly in Google Sheets for data analysis and text tasks
bestFor: Running AI analysis on spreadsheet data directly inside Google Sheets
uniHack: Install the extension and use CLAUDE() formulas to classify, summarize, or extract data from each row automatically
description: Claude for Sheets is a Google Workspace extension that lets students use Claude AI directly in Google Sheets through a CLAUDE() formula. This enables batch processing of text data, sentiment classification of survey responses, and information extraction from messy datasets without leaving the spreadsheet. It requires a Claude API key but the free tier is sufficient for student projects.

name: Tome for Students
tagline: Visual storytelling and pitch deck tool with AI narrative building
bestFor: Creating compelling narrative presentations for pitches and thesis defenses
uniHack: Use Tome to structure your thesis argument visually before writing the full document to ensure logical flow
description: Tome's narrative-focused presentation format helps students structure complex academic arguments and research findings into compelling visual stories. The AI narrative builder suggests how to sequence information for maximum impact, which is useful for thesis defenses and project pitch presentations. The free student tier provides sufficient features for academic presentations.

name: Docusign AI
tagline: AI-powered document analysis and e-signature for student agreements
bestFor: Reviewing and signing internship offers, leases, and academic agreements
uniHack: Use the free envelope per month to sign internship offer letters or research participant consent forms digitally
description: Docusign allows students to sign important documents like internship offers, apartment leases, and research consent forms digitally from any device. Its AI contract review feature highlights key terms and unusual clauses in agreements before signing. The free tier covers one sent document per month, sufficient for occasional student use.

name: ChatPDF
tagline: Upload any PDF and chat with it to get instant answers
bestFor: Getting quick answers from research papers and textbook chapters
uniHack: Ask the paper its own limitations to quickly assess research quality for your literature review
description: ChatPDF allows students to upload any PDF and immediately chat with it to extract information, summaries, and answers with precise page citations. It is one of the most popular free PDF chat tools with three free document conversations per day. Students use it for research papers, textbook chapters, lecture slides, and any academic PDF material.

name: Gamma Docs
tagline: Create beautiful documents and reports with AI-designed layouts
bestFor: Creating visually polished research reports and project documents
uniHack: Generate your project proposal as a Gamma doc to impress professors with visual design beyond a plain Word document
description: Gamma's document mode creates visually rich reports and proposals that go beyond standard text documents with professional card-based layouts, embedded media, and visual data displays. Students use it to present research proposals and project documentation in a polished format that stands out. Documents are shared as web links with real-time viewing analytics.

name: Stable Audio
tagline: AI music and sound effect generator for creative projects
bestFor: Generating background music and sound effects for video and media projects
uniHack: Generate specific sound effects like 'rain on a window' or 'busy coffee shop ambiance' for your podcast or film
description: Stable Audio from Stability AI generates high-quality music and sound effects from text prompts, supporting precise control over genre, mood, BPM, and duration. Students producing podcasts, short films, or multimedia projects use it to generate custom royalty-free audio quickly. The free tier provides 20 track generations per month without a subscription.

name: Krea AI
tagline: Real-time AI image generation and enhancement for creative workflows
bestFor: Real-time visual brainstorming and image enhancement for design projects
uniHack: Use the real-time canvas to sketch rough concept ideas and see AI generate variations instantly as you draw
description: Krea AI offers a unique real-time image generation canvas where the AI updates the generated image as the student types or draws, enabling rapid visual ideation. Its upscale and enhance tools can significantly improve the quality of any existing image. Design students use the real-time canvas for quick concept visualization and the enhancement tools to refine final project images.
